title: Porophyllum suffruticosum
taxonomy: Porophyllum suffruticosum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants with composite flowers. Porophyllum suffruticosum is further classified under the genus Porophyllum, which includes several species of shrubby plants native to the Americas. The genus Porophyllum is part of the tribe Heliantheae, a group of flowering plants characterized by their daisy-like flowers. Overall, Porophyllum suffruticosum's classification reflects its botanical relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Porophyllum suffruticosum, also known as Yerba de San Juan, is a small, shrub-like plant belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Americas. The health benefits of Porophyllum suffruticosum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, making it a valuable treatment for various ailments such as gastrointestinal issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Yerba de San Juan is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
